options.description: Show version information

options.name: -h
options.description: Show help and usage information

options.name: --help-internal
options.description: Show help and usage information

commands.name: resolve
commands.description: Only check for parse and type resolution errors.

options.name: --show-snippets
options.description: Show a source code snippet for each Dafny message.

options.description:
Print Dafny program after parsing it.

options.description:
Print Dafny program after resolving it.

options.description:
Print Boogie splits translated from Dafny

Print passified Boogie program translated from Dafny

options.name: --bprint
options.description:
Print Boogie program translated from Dafny

options.name: --print-mode
options.description:
Everything - Print everything listed below.
Serialization - print the source that will be included in a compiled dll.
NoIncludes - disable printing of {:verify false} methods
incorporated via the include mechanism, as well as datatypes and
fields included from other files.
NoGhost - disable printing of functions, ghost methods, and proof
statements in implementation methods. It also disables anything
NoIncludes disables.

options.name: --allow-warnings
options.description: Allow compilation to continue and succeed when warnings occur. Errors will still halt and fail compilation.

options.name: --warn-as-errors
options.description: (Deprecated). Please use --allow-warnings instead

options.name: --show-hints
options.description: Show hints that might help you better understand your code, such as what triggers Dafny generators for quantifiers

options.name: --warn-shadowing
options.description: Emits a warning if the name of a declared variable caused another variable to be shadowed.

options.name: --warn-missing-constructor-parentheses
options.description: Emits a warning when a constructor name in a case pattern is not followed by parentheses.

options.name: --track-print-effects
options.description: A compiled method, constructor, or iterator is allowed to have print effects only if it is marked with {{:print}}.

options.name: --allow-axioms
options.description: Prevents a warning from being generated for axioms, such as assume statements and functions or methods without a body, that don't have an {:axiom} attribute.

options.name: --enforce-determinism
options.description: Check that only deterministic statements are used, so that values seen during execution will be the same in every run of the program.

options.name: --allow-external-contracts
options.description: Allow exporting callables with preconditions, and importing callables with postconditions

options.name: --find-project
options.description: Uses the first specified input file as a starting path, from which to search for a project file by traversing up the file tree.

options.name: --print-ranges
options.description: Prints not just the center, but also the start and end of diagnostics

options.name: --wait-for-debugger
options.description: Lets the C# compiler block until a .NET debugger is attached

options.name: --ignore-indentation
options.description: Do not report warnings for suspicious indentation

options.name: --stdin
options.description: Read standard input and treat it as an input .dfy file.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print additional information such as which files are emitted where.

options.name: --cores
options.description: Run the Dafny verifier using <n> cores, or using <XX%> of the machine's logical cores.

options.name: --library
options.description:
The contents of this file and any files it includes can be referenced from other files as if they were included.
However, these contents are skipped during code generation and verification.
This option is useful in a diamond dependency situation,
to prevent code from the bottom dependency from being generated more than once.
The value may be a comma-separated list of files and folders.

options.name: --allow-deprecation
options.description: Do not warn about the use of deprecated features.

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
(experimental) One path to an assembly that contains at least one
instantiatable class extending Microsoft.Dafny.Plugin.Rewriter. It
can also extend Microsoft.Dafny.Plugins.PluginConfiguration to
receive arguments. More information about what plugins do and how
to define them:
https://github.com/dafny-lang/dafny/blob/master/Source/DafnyLanguageServer/README.md#about-plugins

options.name: --prelude
options.description: Choose the Dafny prelude file.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --unicode-char
options.description:
false - The char type represents any UTF-16 code unit.
true - The char type represents any Unicode scalar value.

options.name: --raw-pointers
options.description:
(backend-specific: Rust)
false - All class instances are reference-counted or garbage collected
true - All class instances are raw pointers and need to be manually deallocated

options.name: --use-basename-for-filename
options.description: When parsing use basename of file for tokens instead of the path supplied on the command line

options.name: --emit-uncompilable-code
options.description: Rather than throwing an exception, allow compilers to emit uncompilable information including what is not compilable instead of regular code. Useful when developing compilers or to document for each test what compiler feature is missing

options.name: --general-traits
options.description:
legacy - Every trait implicitly extends 'object', and thus is a reference type. Only traits and reference types can extend traits.
datatype (default) - A trait is a reference type only if it or one of its ancestor traits is 'object'. Any non-'newtype' type with members can extend traits.
full - (don't use; not yet completely supported) A trait is a reference type only if it or one of its ancestor traits is 'object'. Any type with members can extend traits.

options.name: --general-newtypes
options.description:
false - A newtype can only be based on numeric types or another newtype.
true (default) - (requires --type-system-refresh) A newtype case be based on any non-reference, non-trait, non-arrow, non-ORDINAL type.

options.name: --type-system-refresh
options.description:
false - The type-inference engine and supported types are those of Dafny 4.0.
true (default) - Use an updated type-inference engine.

options.name: --type-inference-trace
options.description:
false - Don't print type-inference debug information.
true - Print type-inference debug information.

options.name: --type-system-debug
options.description:
false - Don't print debug information for the new type system.
true - Print debug information for the new type system.

options.name: --reads-clauses-on-methods
options.description: Allows reads clauses on methods (with a default of 'reads *') as well as functions.

options.name: --standard-libraries
options.description:
Allow Dafny code to depend on the standard libraries included with the Dafny distribution.
See https://github.com/dafny-lang/dafny/blob/master/Source/DafnyStandardLibraries/README.md for more information.
Not compatible with the --unicode-char:false option.

options.name: --log-level
options.description: Sets the level at which events are logged

options.name: --log-location
options.description: Sets the directory where to store log files

options.name: --check-source-location-consistency
options.description: Check that parent nodes contain their children

options.name: --referrers
options.description: Enables the modeling of backreferences in Dafny, so that one can reason about memory locations at which objects are being stored.

arguments.name: FILE
arguments.description: Dafny input files and/or a Dafny project file

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify the program.

options.name: --performance-stats
options.description: Report a summary of the verification performance. The given argument is used to divide all the output with, which can help ignore small differences.

options.name: --filter-symbol
options.description: Filter what gets verified by selecting only symbols whose fully qualified name contains the given argument, for example: "--filter-symbol=MyNestedModule.MyFooFunction". Place a dot at the end of the argument to indicate the symbol name must end like this, which can be useful if one symbol name is a prefix of another.

options.name: --filter-position
options.description: Filter what gets verified based on a source location. The location is specified as a file path suffix, optionally followed by a colon and a line number or line range. For example, `dafny verify dfyconfig.toml --filter-position=source1.dfy:5-7` will only verify things that between (and including) line 5 and 7 in the file `source1.dfy`. You can also use `:5`, `:5-`, `:-5` to specify individual lines or open ranges. In combination with `--isolate-assertions`, individual assertions can be verified by filtering on the line that contains them. When processing a single file, the filename can be skipped, for example: `dafny verify MyFile.dfy --filter-position=:23`

options.name: --dont-verify-dependencies
options.description: Allows Dafny to accept dependencies that may not have been previously verified, which can be useful during development.

options.name: --progress
options.description: While verifying, output information that helps track progress. Use 'Symbol' to show progress across symbols such as methods and functions. Verification of a symbol may be split across several assertion batches. Use Batch to additionally show progress across batches.

options.name: --relax-definite-assignment
options.description: Allow variables to be read before they are assigned, but only if they have an auto-initializable type or if they are ghost and have a nonempty type.

options.name: --verification-time-limit
options.description: Limit the number of seconds spent trying to verify each assertion batch. A value of 0 indicates no limit

options.name: --verify-included-files
options.description: Verify code in included files.

options.name: --manual-lemma-induction
options.description: Turn off automatic induction for lemmas.

options.name: --solver-path
options.description: Can be used to specify a custom SMT solver to use for verifying Dafny proofs.

options.name: --disable-nonlinear-arithmetic
options.description:
(experimental, will be replaced in the future)
Reduce Dafny's knowledge of non-linear arithmetic (*,/,%).
Results in more manual work, but also produces more predictable behavior.

options.name: --isolate-assertions
options.description: Verify each assertion in isolation.

options.name: --boogie
options.description: Specify arguments that are passed to Boogie, a tool used to verify Dafny programs.

options.name: --log-format
--resource-limit
options.description: Specify the maximum resource limit (rlimit) value to pass to Z3. A resource limit is a deterministic alternative to a time limit. The output produced by `--log-format csv` includes the resource use of each proof effort, which you can use to determine an appropriate limit for your program.

options.name: --solver-option
options.description: Specify an option to control how the solver works. Use --solver-option-help for details on possible parameters. Note that this directly changes the internal behavior of Boogie, and some option settings do not work well or at all with Dafny. More information about Boogie can be found at https://github.com/boogie-org/boogie.

options.name: --solver-option-help
options.description: Describe the possible parameters to --solver-option.

options.name: --solver-plugin
options.description: Dafny uses Boogie as part of its verification process. This option allows customising that part using a Boogie plugin. More information about Boogie can be found at https://github.com/boogie-org/boogie. Information on how to construct Boogie plugins can be found by looking at the code in https://github.com/boogie-org/boogie/blob/v2.16.3/Source/Provers/SMTLib/ProverUtil.cs#L316

options.name: --solver-log
options.description: Specify a file to use to log the SMT-Lib text sent to the solver.

options.name: --json-output
options.description: Return output in a JSON format.

options.name: --json-diagnostics
options.description: Deprecated. Return diagnostics in a JSON format.

options.name: --error-limit
options.description: Set the maximum number of errors to report (0 for unlimited).

options.name: --default-function-opacity
options.description:
Change the default opacity of functions.
`transparent` (default) means functions are transparent, can be manually made opaque and then revealed.
`autoRevealDependencies` makes all functions not explicitly labelled as opaque to be opaque but reveals them automatically in scopes which do not have `{:autoRevealDependencies false}`.
`opaque` means functions are always opaque so the opaque keyword is not needed, and functions must be revealed everywhere needed for a proof.

options.name: --warn-contradictory-assumptions
options.description:
(experimental) Emits a warning if any assertions are proved based on contradictory assumptions (vacuously).
May slow down verification slightly, or make it more brittle.
May produce spurious warnings.
Use the `{:contradiction}` attribute to mark any `assert` statement intended to be part of a proof by contradiction.

options.name: --warn-redundant-assumptions
options.description:
(experimental) Emits a warning if any `requires` clause or `assume` statement was not needed to complete verification.
May slow down verification slightly, or make it more brittle.
May produce spurious warnings.

options.name: --suggest-proof-refactoring
options.description:
(experimental) Emits suggestions for moving assertions into by-proofs and hiding unused function definitions.
May produce spurious suggestions. Use with --show-hints on the CLI.

options.name: --analyze-proofs
options.description:
Uses data from the verifier to suggest ways to refine the proof:
Warning if any assertions are proved based on contradictory assumptions (vacuously).
Warning if any `requires` clause or `assume` statement was not needed to complete verification.
Suggestions for moving assertions into by-proofs and hiding unused function definitions.
May slow down verification slightly, or make it more brittle.
May produce spurious warnings.
Use the `{:contradiction}` attribute to mark any `assert` statement intended to be part of a proof by contradiction.

options.name: --no-timestamp-for-coverage-report
options.description: Write coverage report directly to the specified folder instead of creating a timestamped subdirectory.

options.name: --verification-coverage-report
options.description: Emit verification coverage report to a given directory, in the same format as a test coverage report.

options.name: --extract-counterexample
options.description: If verification fails, report a detailed counterexample for the first failing assertion (experimental).

options.name: --manual-triggers
options.description: Do not generate {:trigger} annotations for user-level quantifiers

options.name: --show-proof-obligation-expressions
options.description: (Experimental) Show Dafny expressions corresponding to unverified proof obligations.

commands.name: build
commands.description: Produce an executable binary or a library.

options.name: --output
options.description: Specify the filename and location for the generated target language files.

options.name: --target
options.description:
cs - Compile to .NET via C#.
go - Compile to Go.
js - Compile to JavaScript.
java - Compile to Java.
py - Compile to Python.
cpp - (experimental) Compile to C++.
Note that the C++ backend has various limitations (see Docs/Compilation/Cpp.md). This includes lack of support for BigIntegers (aka int), most higher order functions, and advanced features like traits or co-inductive types.

options.name: --spill-translation
options.description: In case the Dafny source code is translated to another language, emit that translation.

options.name: --include-runtime
options.description: Include the Dafny runtime as source in the target language.

options.name: --coverage-report
options.description: Emit execution coverage report to a given directory.

options.name: --no-verify
options.description: Skip verification

options.name: --hidden-no-verify
options.description: Allows building unverified libraries without recording that they were not verified.

options.name: --optimize-erasable-datatype-wrapper
options.description:
false - Include all non-ghost datatype constructors in the compiled code
true - In the compiled target code, transform any non-extern
datatype with a single non-ghost constructor that has a single
non-ghost parameter into just that parameter. For example, the type
datatype Record = Record(x: int)
is transformed into just 'int' in the target code.

options.name: --test-assumptions
options.description:
(experimental) When turned on, inserts runtime tests at locations where (implicit) assumptions occur, such as when calling or being called by external code and when using assume statements.
Functionality is still being expanded. Currently only checks contracts on every call to a function or method marked with the {:extern} attribute.

options.name: --bootstrapping
options.description:
(internal, may be removed in the future)
Indicates the Dafny source is part of the Dafny implementation itself,
enabling necessary special handling.

options.name: --compile-suffix
options.description: Add the suffix _Compile to module names without :extern

options.name: --system-module
options.description: How to handle the built-in _System module.

options.name: --translation-record
options.description: A translation record file for previously translated Dafny code. Can be specified multiple times. See https://dafny.org/dafny/DafnyRef/DafnyRef#sec-dtr-files for details.

options.name: --legacy-module-names
options.description: Generate module names in the older A_mB_mC style instead of the current A.B.C scheme

options.name: --translate-standard-library
options.description: When translating Dafny code to another language, Dafny will, for now, include the standard library as if these were source files. This causes conflicts when multiple such translated projects are combined. When combining such projects, please ensure that only one of them has --translate-standard-library set to true.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

commands.description: Translate Dafny sources to ResolvedDesugaredExecutableDafny source and build files.

options.name: --output
options.description: Specify the filename and location for the generated target language files.

options.name: --outer-module
options.description: Nest all code in this module. Can be used to customize generated code. Use dots as separators (foo.baz.zoo) for deeper nesting. The first specified module will be the outermost one.

options.name: --include-runtime
options.description: Include the Dafny runtime as source in the target language.

options.name: --include-test-runner
options.description: Include a program entry point that will run all methods marked with {:test}

options.name: --translation-record-output
options.description: Where to output the translation record file. Defaults to the output directory. See https://dafny.org/dafny/DafnyRef/DafnyRef#sec-dtr-files for details.

options.name: --no-verify
options.description: Skip verification

options.name: --hidden-no-verify
options.description: Allows building unverified libraries without recording that they were not verified.

options.name: --optimize-erasable-datatype-wrapper
options.description:
false - Include all non-ghost datatype constructors in the compiled code
true - In the compiled target code, transform any non-extern
datatype with a single non-ghost constructor that has a single
non-ghost parameter into just that parameter. For example, the type
datatype Record = Record(x: int)
is transformed into just 'int' in the target code.

options.name: --test-assumptions
options.description:
(experimental) When turned on, inserts runtime tests at locations where (implicit) assumptions occur, such as when calling or being called by external code and when using assume statements.
Functionality is still being expanded. Currently only checks contracts on every call to a function or method marked with the {:extern} attribute.

options.name: --bootstrapping
options.description:
(internal, may be removed in the future)
Indicates the Dafny source is part of the Dafny implementation itself,
enabling necessary special handling.

options.name: --compile-suffix
options.description: Add the suffix _Compile to module names without :extern

options.name: --system-module
options.description: How to handle the built-in _System module.

options.name: --translation-record
options.description: A translation record file for previously translated Dafny code. Can be specified multiple times. See https://dafny.org/dafny/DafnyRef/DafnyRef#sec-dtr-files for details.

options.name: --legacy-module-names
options.description: Generate module names in the older A_mB_mC style instead of the current A.B.C scheme

options.name: --translate-standard-library
options.description: When translating Dafny code to another language, Dafny will, for now, include the standard library as if these were source files. This causes conflicts when multiple such translated projects are combined. When combining such projects, please ensure that only one of them has --translate-standard-library set to true.

options.name: --progress
options.description: While verifying, output information that helps track progress. Use 'Symbol' to show progress across symbols such as methods and functions. Verification of a symbol may be split across several assertion batches. Use Batch to additionally show progress across batches.

options.name: --relax-definite-assignment
options.description: Allow variables to be read before they are assigned, but only if they have an auto-initializable type or if they are ghost and have a nonempty type.

options.name: --verification-time-limit
options.description: Limit the number of seconds spent trying to verify each assertion batch. A value of 0 indicates no limit

options.name: --verify-included-files
options.description: Verify code in included files.

options.name: --manual-lemma-induction
options.description: Turn off automatic induction for lemmas.

options.name: --solver-path
options.description: Can be used to specify a custom SMT solver to use for verifying Dafny proofs.

options.name: --disable-nonlinear-arithmetic
options.description:
(experimental, will be replaced in the future)
Reduce Dafny's knowledge of non-linear arithmetic (*,/,%).
Results in more manual work, but also produces more predictable behavior.

options.name: --isolate-assertions
options.description: Verify each assertion in isolation.

options.name: --boogie
options.description: Specify arguments that are passed to Boogie, a tool used to verify Dafny programs.

options.name: --log-format
--resource-limit
options.description: Specify the maximum resource limit (rlimit) value to pass to Z3. A resource limit is a deterministic alternative to a time limit. The output produced by `--log-format csv` includes the resource use of each proof effort, which you can use to determine an appropriate limit for your program.

options.name: --solver-option
options.description: Specify an option to control how the solver works. Use --solver-option-help for details on possible parameters. Note that this directly changes the internal behavior of Boogie, and some option settings do not work well or at all with Dafny. More information about Boogie can be found at https://github.com/boogie-org/boogie.

options.name: --solver-option-help
options.description: Describe the possible parameters to --solver-option.

options.name: --solver-plugin
options.description: Dafny uses Boogie as part of its verification process. This option allows customising that part using a Boogie plugin. More information about Boogie can be found at https://github.com/boogie-org/boogie. Information on how to construct Boogie plugins can be found by looking at the code in https://github.com/boogie-org/boogie/blob/v2.16.3/Source/Provers/SMTLib/ProverUtil.cs#L316

options.name: --solver-log
options.description: Specify a file to use to log the SMT-Lib text sent to the solver.

options.name: --json-output
options.description: Return output in a JSON format.

options.name: --json-diagnostics
options.description: Deprecated. Return diagnostics in a JSON format.

options.name: --error-limit
options.description: Set the maximum number of errors to report (0 for unlimited).

options.name: --default-function-opacity
options.description:
Change the default opacity of functions.
`transparent` (default) means functions are transparent, can be manually made opaque and then revealed.
`autoRevealDependencies` makes all functions not explicitly labelled as opaque to be opaque but reveals them automatically in scopes which do not have `{:autoRevealDependencies false}`.
`opaque` means functions are always opaque so the opaque keyword is not needed, and functions must be revealed everywhere needed for a proof.

options.name: --warn-contradictory-assumptions
options.description:
(experimental) Emits a warning if any assertions are proved based on contradictory assumptions (vacuously).
May slow down verification slightly, or make it more brittle.
May produce spurious warnings.
Use the `{:contradiction}` attribute to mark any `assert` statement intended to be part of a proof by contradiction.

options.name: --warn-redundant-assumptions
options.description:
(experimental) Emits a warning if any `requires` clause or `assume` statement was not needed to complete verification.
May slow down verification slightly, or make it more brittle.
May produce spurious warnings.

options.name: --suggest-proof-refactoring
options.description:
(experimental) Emits suggestions for moving assertions into by-proofs and hiding unused function definitions.
May produce spurious suggestions. Use with --show-hints on the CLI.

options.name: --analyze-proofs
options.description:
Uses data from the verifier to suggest ways to refine the proof:
Warning if any assertions are proved based on contradictory assumptions (vacuously).
Warning if any `requires` clause or `assume` statement was not needed to complete verification.
Suggestions for moving assertions into by-proofs and hiding unused function definitions.
May slow down verification slightly, or make it more brittle.
May produce spurious warnings.
Use the `{:contradiction}` attribute to mark any `assert` statement intended to be part of a proof by contradiction.

options.name: --no-timestamp-for-coverage-report
options.description: Write coverage report directly to the specified folder instead of creating a timestamped subdirectory.

options.name: --verification-coverage-report
options.description: Emit verification coverage report to a given directory, in the same format as a test coverage report.

options.name: --extract-counterexample
options.description: If verification fails, report a detailed counterexample for the first failing assertion (experimental).

options.name: --manual-triggers
options.description: Do not generate {:trigger} annotations for user-level quantifiers

options.name: --show-proof-obligation-expressions
options.description: (Experimental) Show Dafny expressions corresponding to unverified proof obligations.

options.name: --show-snippets
options.description: Show a source code snippet for each Dafny message.

options.name: --print
options.description:
Print Dafny program after parsing it.
(Use - as <file> to print to console.)

options.name: --rprint
options.description:
Print Dafny program after resolving it.
(use - as <file> to print to console.)

options.name: --sprint
options.description:
Print Boogie splits translated from Dafny
(use - as <file> to print to console)

options.name: --pprint
options.description:
Print passified Boogie program translated from Dafny
(use - as <file> to print to console)

options.name: --bprint
options.description:
Print Boogie program translated from Dafny
(use - as <file> to print to console)

options.name: --print-mode
options.description:
Everything - Print everything listed below.
Serialization - print the source that will be included in a compiled dll.
NoIncludes - disable printing of {:verify false} methods
incorporated via the include mechanism, as well as datatypes and
fields included from other files.
NoGhost - disable printing of functions, ghost methods, and proof
statements in implementation methods. It also disables anything
NoIncludes disables.

options.name: --allow-warnings
options.description: Allow compilation to continue and succeed when warnings occur. Errors will still halt and fail compilation.

options.name: --warn-as-errors
options.description: (Deprecated). Please use --allow-warnings instead

options.name: --show-hints
options.description: Show hints that might help you better understand your code, such as what triggers Dafny generators for quantifiers

options.name: --warn-shadowing
options.description: Emits a warning if the name of a declared variable caused another variable to be shadowed.

options.name: --warn-missing-constructor-parentheses
options.description: Emits a warning when a constructor name in a case pattern is not followed by parentheses.

options.name: --track-print-effects
options.description: A compiled method, constructor, or iterator is allowed to have print effects only if it is marked with {{:print}}.

options.name: --allow-axioms
options.description: Prevents a warning from being generated for axioms, such as assume statements and functions or methods without a body, that don't have an {:axiom} attribute.

options.name: --enforce-determinism
options.description: Check that only deterministic statements are used, so that values seen during execution will be the same in every run of the program.

options.name: --allow-external-contracts
options.description: Allow exporting callables with preconditions, and importing callables with postconditions

options.name: --find-project
options.description: Uses the first specified input file as a starting path, from which to search for a project file by traversing up the file tree.

options.name: --print-ranges
options.description: Prints not just the center, but also the start and end of diagnostics

options.name: --wait-for-debugger
options.description: Lets the C# compiler block until a .NET debugger is attached

options.name: --ignore-indentation
options.description: Do not report warnings for suspicious indentation

options.name: --stdin
options.description: Read standard input and treat it as an input .dfy file.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print additional information such as which files are emitted where.

options.name: --cores
options.description: Run the Dafny verifier using <n> cores, or using <XX%> of the machine's logical cores.

options.name: --library
options.description:
The contents of this file and any files it includes can be referenced from other files as if they were included.
However, these contents are skipped during code generation and verification.
This option is useful in a diamond dependency situation,
to prevent code from the bottom dependency from being generated more than once.
The value may be a comma-separated list of files and folders.

options.name: --allow-deprecation
options.description: Do not warn about the use of deprecated features.

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
(experimental) One path to an assembly that contains at least one
instantiatable class extending Microsoft.Dafny.Plugin.Rewriter. It
can also extend Microsoft.Dafny.Plugins.PluginConfiguration to
receive arguments. More information about what plugins do and how
to define them:
https://github.com/dafny-lang/dafny/blob/master/Source/DafnyLanguageServer/README.md#about-plugins

options.name: --prelude
options.description: Choose the Dafny prelude file.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --unicode-char
options.description:
false - The char type represents any UTF-16 code unit.
true - The char type represents any Unicode scalar value.

options.name: --raw-pointers
options.description:
(backend-specific: Rust)
false - All class instances are reference-counted or garbage collected
true - All class instances are raw pointers and need to be manually deallocated

options.name: --use-basename-for-filename
options.description: When parsing use basename of file for tokens instead of the path supplied on the command line

options.name: --emit-uncompilable-code
options.description: Rather than throwing an exception, allow compilers to emit uncompilable information including what is not compilable instead of regular code. Useful when developing compilers or to document for each test what compiler feature is missing

options.name: --general-traits
options.description:
legacy - Every trait implicitly extends 'object', and thus is a reference type. Only traits and reference types can extend traits.
datatype (default) - A trait is a reference type only if it or one of its ancestor traits is 'object'. Any non-'newtype' type with members can extend traits.
full - (don't use; not yet completely supported) A trait is a reference type only if it or one of its ancestor traits is 'object'. Any type with members can extend traits.

options.name: --general-newtypes
options.description:
false - A newtype can only be based on numeric types or another newtype.
true (default) - (requires --type-system-refresh) A newtype case be based on any non-reference, non-trait, non-arrow, non-ORDINAL type.

options.name: --type-system-refresh
options.description:
false - The type-inference engine and supported types are those of Dafny 4.0.
true (default) - Use an updated type-inference engine.

options.name: --type-inference-trace
options.description:
false - Don't print type-inference debug information.
true - Print type-inference debug information.

options.name: --type-system-debug
options.description:
false - Don't print debug information for the new type system.
true - Print debug information for the new type system.

options.name: --reads-clauses-on-methods
options.description: Allows reads clauses on methods (with a default of 'reads *') as well as functions.

options.name: --standard-libraries
options.description:
Allow Dafny code to depend on the standard libraries included with the Dafny distribution.
See https://github.com/dafny-lang/dafny/blob/master/Source/DafnyStandardLibraries/README.md for more information.
Not compatible with the --unicode-char:false option.

options.name: --log-level
options.description: Sets the level at which events are logged

options.name: --log-location
options.description: Sets the directory where to store log files

options.name: --check-source-location-consistency
options.description: Check that parent nodes contain their children

options.name: --referrers
options.description: Enables the modeling of backreferences in Dafny, so that one can reason about memory locations at which objects are being stored.

options.name: --random-seed
options.description: Turn on randomization of the input that Dafny passes to the SMT solver and turn on randomization in the SMT solver itself. Certain Dafny proofs are complex in the sense that changes to the proof that preserve its meaning may cause its verification result to change. This option simulates meaning-preserving changes to the proofs without requiring the user to actually make those changes. The proof changes are renaming variables and reordering declarations in the SMT input passed to the solver, and setting solver options that have similar effects.

options.description: Filter what gets verified by selecting only symbols whose fully qualified name contains the given argument, for example: "--filter-symbol=MyNestedModule.MyFooFunction". Place a dot at the end of the argument to indicate the symbol name must end like this, which can be useful if one symbol name is a prefix of another.

options.name: --filter-position
options.description: Filter what gets verified based on a source location. The location is specified as a file path suffix, optionally followed by a colon and a line number or line range. For example, `dafny verify dfyconfig.toml --filter-position=source1.dfy:5-7` will only verify things that between (and including) line 5 and 7 in the file `source1.dfy`. You can also use `:5`, `:5-`, `:-5` to specify individual lines or open ranges. In combination with `--isolate-assertions`, individual assertions can be verified by filtering on the line that contains them. When processing a single file, the filename can be skipped, for example: `dafny verify MyFile.dfy --filter-position=:23`

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

Print passified Boogie program translated from Dafny
(use - as <file> to print to console)

options.name: --bprint
options.description:
Print Boogie program translated from Dafny
(use - as <file> to print to console)

options.name: --print-mode
options.description:
Everything - Print everything listed below.
Serialization - print the source that will be included in a compiled dll.
NoIncludes - disable printing of {:verify false} methods
incorporated via the include mechanism, as well as datatypes and
fields included from other files.
NoGhost - disable printing of functions, ghost methods, and proof
statements in implementation methods. It also disables anything
NoIncludes disables.

options.name: --allow-warnings
options.description: Allow compilation to continue and succeed when warnings occur. Errors will still halt and fail compilation.

options.name: --warn-as-errors
options.description: (Deprecated). Please use --allow-warnings instead

options.name: --show-hints
options.description: Show hints that might help you better understand your code, such as what triggers Dafny generators for quantifiers

options.name: --warn-shadowing
options.description: Emits a warning if the name of a declared variable caused another variable to be shadowed.

options.name: --warn-missing-constructor-parentheses
options.description: Emits a warning when a constructor name in a case pattern is not followed by parentheses.

options.name: --track-print-effects
options.description: A compiled method, constructor, or iterator is allowed to have print effects only if it is marked with {{:print}}.

options.name: --allow-axioms
options.description: Prevents a warning from being generated for axioms, such as assume statements and functions or methods without a body, that don't have an {:axiom} attribute.

options.name: --enforce-determinism
options.description: Check that only deterministic statements are used, so that values seen during execution will be the same in every run of the program.

options.name: --allow-external-contracts
options.description: Allow exporting callables with preconditions, and importing callables with postconditions

options.name: --find-project
options.description: Uses the first specified input file as a starting path, from which to search for a project file by traversing up the file tree.

options.name: --print-ranges
options.description: Prints not just the center, but also the start and end of diagnostics

options.name: --wait-for-debugger
options.description: Lets the C# compiler block until a .NET debugger is attached

options.name: --ignore-indentation
options.description: Do not report warnings for suspicious indentation

options.name: --stdin
options.description: Read standard input and treat it as an input .dfy file.

options.name: --verbose
options.description: Print additional information such as which files are emitted where.

options.name: --cores
options.description: Run the Dafny verifier using <n> cores, or using <XX%> of the machine's logical cores.

options.name: --library
options.description:
The contents of this file and any files it includes can be referenced from other files as if they were included.
However, these contents are skipped during code generation and verification.
This option is useful in a diamond dependency situation,
to prevent code from the bottom dependency from being generated more than once.
The value may be a comma-separated list of files and folders.

options.name: --allow-deprecation
options.description: Do not warn about the use of deprecated features.

options.name: --plugin
options.description:
(experimental) One path to an assembly that contains at least one
instantiatable class extending Microsoft.Dafny.Plugin.Rewriter. It
can also extend Microsoft.Dafny.Plugins.PluginConfiguration to
receive arguments. More information about what plugins do and how
to define them:
https://github.com/dafny-lang/dafny/blob/master/Source/DafnyLanguageServer/README.md#about-plugins

options.name: --prelude
options.description: Choose the Dafny prelude file.

options.name: --function-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for functions changed from Dafny version 3 to version 4. This switch controls access to the new syntax, and also provides a mode to help with migration.
3 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `function` and `predicate`.
4 - Compiled functions are written `function` and `predicate`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`.
migration3to4 - Compiled functions are written `function method` and `predicate method`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. To migrate from version 3 to version 4, use this flag on your version 3 program. This will give flag all occurrences of `function` and `predicate` as parsing errors. These are ghost functions, so change those into the new syntax `ghost function` and `ghost predicate`. Then, start using --functionSyntax:4. This will flag all occurrences of `function method` and `predicate method` as parsing errors. So, change those to just `function` and `predicate`. Now, your program uses version 4 syntax and has the exact same meaning as your previous version 3 program.
experimentalDefaultGhost - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring ghost functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalDefaultCompiled - Like migration3to4, but allow `function` and `predicate` as alternatives to declaring compiled
functions and predicates, respectively.
experimentalPredicateAlwaysGhost - Compiled functions are written `function`. Ghost functions are written `ghost function`. Predicates are always ghost and are written `predicate`.

options.name: --quantifier-syntax
options.description:
The syntax for quantification domains is changing from Dafny version 3 to version 4, more specifically where quantifier ranges (|
<Range>) are allowed. This switch gives early access to the new syntax.
3 - Ranges are only allowed after all quantified variables are declared.
(e.g. set x, y | 0 <= x < |s| && y in s[x] && 0 <= y :: y)
4 - Ranges are allowed after each quantified variable declaration.
(e.g. set x | 0 <= x < |s|, y <- s[x] | 0 <= y :: y)
Note that quantifier variable domains (<- <Domain>) are available in both syntax versions.

options.name: --unicode-char
options.description:
false - The char type represents any UTF-16 code unit.
true - The char type represents any Unicode scalar value.

options.name: --raw-pointers
options.description:
(backend-specific: Rust)
false - All class instances are reference-counted or garbage collected
true - All class instances are raw pointers and need to be manually deallocated

options.name: --use-basename-for-filename
options.description: When parsing use basename of file for tokens instead of the path supplied on the command line

options.name: --emit-uncompilable-code
options.description: Rather than throwing an exception, allow compilers to emit uncompilable information including what is not compilable instead of regular code. Useful when developing compilers or to document for each test what compiler feature is missing

options.name: --general-traits
options.description:
legacy - Every trait implicitly extends 'object', and thus is a reference type. Only traits and reference types can extend traits.
datatype (default) - A trait is a reference type only if it or one of its ancestor traits is 'object'. Any non-'newtype' type with members can extend traits.
full - (don't use; not yet completely supported) A trait is a reference type only if it or one of its ancestor traits is 'object'. Any type with members can extend traits.

options.name: --general-newtypes
options.description:
false - A newtype can only be based on numeric types or another newtype.
true (default) - (requires --type-system-refresh) A newtype case be based on any non-reference, non-trait, non-arrow, non-ORDINAL type.

options.name: --type-system-refresh
options.description:
false - The type-inference engine and supported types are those of Dafny 4.0.
true (default) - Use an updated type-inference engine.

options.name: --type-inference-trace
options.description:
false - Don't print type-inference debug information.
true - Print type-inference debug information.

options.name: --type-system-debug
options.description:
false - Don't print debug information for the new type system.
true - Print debug information for the new type system.

options.name: --reads-clauses-on-methods
options.description: Allows reads clauses on methods (with a default of 'reads *') as well as functions.

options.name: --standard-libraries
options.description:
Allow Dafny code to depend on the standard libraries included with the Dafny distribution.
See https://github.com/dafny-lang/dafny/blob/master/Source/DafnyStandardLibraries/README.md for more information.
Not compatible with the --unicode-char:false option.

options.name: --log-level
options.description: Sets the level at which events are logged

options.name: --log-location
options.description: Sets the directory where to store log files

options.name: --check-source-location-consistency
options.description: Check that parent nodes contain their children

options.name: --referrers
options.description: Enables the modeling of backreferences in Dafny, so that one can reason about memory locations at which objects are being stored.
